goal: Build a simple Python GUI todo list app using tkinter. Features: add tasks, mark complete, delete tasks, persistent storage using JSON file. Single file app.py with a clean modern UI.

comp_001.name: Config
comp_001.description: Defines application-wide constants and configuration values. This module acts as the single source of truth for UI colors, window dimensions, and file paths for persistence, ensuring consistency across the entire GUI and data layer.

comp_002.name: TaskModel
comp_002.description: Defines the core Task data structure using dataclasses for type safety. It encapsulates the properties of a single todo item, including its content, completion status, and unique identifier for state management.

functions: Task.__init__(self, id: str, title: str, completed: bool = False) -> None

comp_003.name: StorageService
comp_003.description: Handles persistent storage of tasks to a JSON file. This service acts as the data access layer, responsible for loading and saving the list of Task objects to ensure data durability across application sessions.

comp_004.name: TodoManager
comp_004.description: Acts as the controller that orchestrates business logic and manages the application state. It maintains the master list of tasks and provides an interface for adding, updating, and removing tasks while coordinating with the storage service and event bus.

functions: TodoManager.__init__(self, storage: StorageManager, bus: EventBus) -> None

comp_005.name: TaskEntryField
comp_005.description: A reusable UI component for user text input. It wraps the tkinter Entry widget to provide a clean interface for typing new task descriptions with integrated validation logic and event handling.

comp_006.name: TaskItemWidget
comp_006.description: A custom GUI component representing a single row in the task list. It displays the task text, a checkbox for completion, and a delete button, publishing events to the bus when interactions occur.

functions: TaskRow.__init__(self, master: any, task: Task, bus: EventBus) -> None

comp_007.name: TaskListView
comp_007.description: A scrollable container that renders the list of active tasks. It manages the layout of multiple TaskItemWidget instances and provides a method to refresh the UI content based on task list state.

comp_008.name: MainApplication
comp_008.description: The primary window class that binds the UI components to the manager. It handles window layout, listens to the event bus for state updates, and triggers refreshing the view.

functions: TodoApp.__init__(self, manager: TodoManager, bus: EventBus) -> None

comp_009.name: Utils
comp_009.description: Provides shared helper functions such as ID generation or simple string manipulation. These utilities keep the domain logic and UI code clean by centralizing common repetitive tasks.

comp_010.name: AppEntry
comp_010.description: The system-managed entry point that initializes the dependency injection container, sets up the event bus, storage connection, launches the manager, and displays the main application window.

comp_011.name: Requirements
comp_011.description: Lists all necessary dependencies for the environment, specifically ensuring that standard library support is met and any external tooling for packaging is declared for the CI/CD pipeline.

comp_012.name: DockerConfig
comp_012.description: Defines the container environment for the application. It captures the operating system dependencies and Python runtime, ensuring that GUI applications can execute via X11 forwarding by configuring DISPLAY environment variables and base image requirements.

comp_013.name: EventBus
comp_013.description: A centralized pub/sub mechanism that decouples UI components from the business logic layer. It allows signals such as 'task-added', 'task-toggled', and 'task-deleted' to be broadcasted and handled without direct coupling.

functions: EventBus.subscribe(self, event_type: str, callback: callable) -> None
EventBus.publish(self, event_type: str, data: any) -> None
